title: asperula glauca subsp. pruinosa
intro: Asperula glauca subsp. pruinosa, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Woodruff include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Woodruff can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
